title: hapalochilus kermesinus
intro: Hapalochilus kermesinus, also known as the red-tipped corydalis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as acting as an antispasmodic and sedative. Hapalochilus kermesinus is often prepared as a tincture or decoction for medicinal use.
